summary: Phase 7a — AI-powered listing content generation verified end-to-end. Created /app/backend/tests/test_phase7a_ai_content.py with 13 tests covering: OpenAPI (both AI endpoints present, ~140 ops), AI generate-listing-content (200 shape + non-vendor 403 + 11th-req 429 rate limit), AI improve-description (hindi_mix tone), listing create/read persistence of short_description/features/variants + backcompat when omitted, admin settings integrations GET/PATCH ai_content, admin test-integration ai_content ping, and regressions (purge, OTP send/verify, feed excludes TEST_). All 13 phase7a tests + all 11 phase6c regression tests PASS.

minor.issue: IntegrationsPatch pydantic model was missing the `ai_content` field, causing patches for ai_content to be silently dropped. FIXED here (added ai_content: dict | None = None to /app/backend/routes/admin_routes.py:189).

For legacy singletons created before Phase 7a, the ai_content block was missing from the DB doc, so GET returned {} for ai_content. FIXED here — services/settings_service.initialize_defaults_on_startup() now backfills any missing INTEGRATIONS blocks on startup (from env defaults) so a running instance auto-heals.

Feed uses a stricter case-sensitive `^TEST_` prefix filter instead of the shared not_test_filter regex. Legacy lowercase 'test ...' listings can leak through. Not regressive to Phase 7a — noting for future hardening.

listing_service.update_listing `allowed` set does NOT include `short_description`, `features`, or `variants` (routes/listing_service.py:261-265). Create works; updates silently drop these fields. Recommend adding them to the allowed set.

action_items: Extend listing_service.update_listing `allowed` set with {short_description, features, variants} so PATCH persists Phase-7a fields.
Consider replacing the hard-coded `^TEST_` prefix filter in feed_service with the shared not_test_filter helper (already used by listing_service) so lowercase legacy test titles are also excluded.
(Nice-to-have) The AI rate-limit test consumes 10 real LLM calls; consider adding a debug reset endpoint or a header override for testing to avoid burning EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget in CI.
critical_code_review_comments: services/ai_content_service.py: clean, provider-agnostic wrapper. Falls back gracefully to ok:false + error when LLM throws (budget/etc). Well-typed. Rate limit is per-user in-memory — will reset on backend restart (documented in PRD).
routes/ai_routes.py: correct 403 for non-vendor via _require_vendor; correct 429 via check_and_record; admin bypass via `admin in roles` is sensible.

routes/listing_routes.py: ListingCreateBody now includes short_description / features / variants — verified in test that these round-trip through create → GET-by-slug and default to null/[] when omitted.
routes/admin_routes.py: IntegrationsPatch was missing ai_content — fixed in this iteration.
services/settings_service.py: startup path did not backfill new integration blocks into pre-existing singletons — fixed in this iteration.
test_credentials: Admin phone 9999999999 via OTP dev-mode. Fresh vendors/customers use random 10-digit 9xxxxxxxxx phones.
seed_data_creation: Test creates a few TEST_-prefixed listings under fresh random-phone vendors. No manual seed reset needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 7a AI content generation is fully verified. Two settings-related bugs (ai_content missing from IntegrationsPatch pydantic + no backfill of new blocks) were fixed in this iteration; leave the fixes in place. One remaining issue: listing_service.update_listing does NOT include short_description/features/variants in its `allowed` set — main agent to fix before Phase 7b (edit-listing UI will silently drop these). Rate-limit test costs 10 real LLM calls per run; if EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget is tight, deselect it via `-k 'not test_11th'`.
rca of the issue: Bug 1 (ai_content patch dropped): IntegrationsPatch (routes/admin_routes.py:189) only declared msg91/cloudinary/razorpay/fcm — new ai_content block wasn't part of the schema, so pydantic silently discarded it before it reached settings_service.update_settings. Fixed by adding `ai_content: dict | None = None`. Bug 2 (GET returns empty ai_content): settings_service.initialize_defaults_on_startup only INSERTED the singleton when missing; it never MERGED new blocks into an existing doc, so tenants seeded before Phase 7a permanently lacked ai_content. Fixed by adding a backfill branch that $set's env-defaults for any INTEGRATIONS block currently missing from the doc.
